raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The artifact makes several inferential leaps that are natural critique targets: it equates a full-curve metric (ROC-AUC) with 'early enrichment', treats direction-consistent gains across only three target classes as sufficient to license the word 'substantial', and elevates a single ablation result to a design recommendation — all of which are logical consistency questions.","R1.3":"There is a direct, load-bearing tension between the stated practical priority (early enrichment at the top 0.5–1% of the ranked list) and the chosen primary metric (ROC-AUC, which integrates true-positive rate over all thresholds and is therefore insensitive to the top-of-list regime). Whether the reported evidence actually supports the early-enrichment claim is the sharpest evidentiary gap in the artifact."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking whether the scientific conclusions are warranted by the reported evidence (logical and evidentiary soundness), rather than whether the contribution is novel relative to prior virtual-screening work?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several inferential leaps that are natural critique targets: it equates a full-curve metric (ROC-AUC) with 'early enrichment', treats direction-consistent gains across only three target classes as sufficient to license the word 'substantial', and elevates a single ablation result to a design recommendation — all of which are logical consistency questions.
rationale.R1.3: There is a direct, load-bearing tension between the stated practical priority (early enrichment at the top 0.5–1% of the ranked list) and the chosen primary metric (ROC-AUC, which integrates true-positive rate over all thresholds and is therefore insensitive to the top-of-list regime). Whether the reported evidence actually supports the early-enrichment claim is the sharpest evidentiary gap in the artifact.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking whether the scientific conclusions are warranted by the reported evidence (logical and evidentiary soundness), rather than whether the contribution is novel relative to prior virtual-screening work?
